summary: Extended the Stage 3 project registry adapter output with bounded TASKS.json candidate task records, project-list exposure, focused tests, and durable planning/handoff evidence.

tests_run.command: uv run --no-sync python -B -m pytest tests/test_projects.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
tests_run.summary: 9 passed; covered registry discovery, candidate task extraction, invalid and oversized TASKS.json handling, CLI JSON and human output, missing-root errors, and path ID normalization.
Planning integrity checks passed with the Stage 3B task claimed for implementation.
Story Loop status reported queue_state=running for the claimed Stage 3B task before completion.
File justification checks passed after registering the new Stage 3B worker-result evidence.
Public repo hygiene checks passed with the Stage 3B public candidate files.
383 passed plus Ruff, format, mypy, CLI smoke checks, file justification, public hygiene, planning integrity, skill inventory, source inventory, protected locks, and uv lock --check.

Project task candidate records expose st.evidence: ProjectTaskCandidate exposes source_id, source_path, title, goal, task_type, acceptance_criteria, verification_commands, allowed_paths, blocked_by, and source_authority; tests assert the normalized fields.

GenericRepoAdapter parses only bounded t.evidence: GenericRepoAdapter reads only the root TASKS.json when it is at or below MAX_TASKS_JSON_BYTES, returns candidate_tasks for valid entries, and reports parse/size failures through adapter_findings; tests cover invalid and oversized files.

project-list --json includes candidate t.evidence: project-list --json serializes ProjectFacts.candidate_tasks, and human output prints candidate_tasks counts; tests cover both outputs and the command remains read-only over project roots.

Focused tests cover valid TASKS.json can.evidence: tests/test_projects.py now contains nine focused tests covering the named behavior and passed with pytest.

Planning SQLite and HANDOFF.md record th.evidence: plans/planning.sqlite3 records the Stage 3B task shape and worker run, and HANDOFF.md records the shaped and completed Stage 3B evidence, checks, risks, and next action.

review.summary: Fresh-thread-style local review found no actionable findings. The new parser is bounded to top-level TASKS.json, reports bad input as adapter findings, and keeps planning-task seeding for a later slice.

risks: Stage 3 is not fully complete: named specialized adapters and actual planning-task seeding remain future slices.
TASKS.json candidate parsing is intentionally conservative and does not validate all future planning-task readiness rules yet.
Live Codex Exec launch remains unavailable in this Windows shell because the resolved WindowsApps codex.exe path returns Access is denied.
follow_up_tasks: Implement the next Stage 3 slice for planning-task seed conversion from candidate tasks.
Add named project adapters after the common candidate-task output contract is stable.
handoff_notes: Stage 3B adds bounded adapter task candidates from top-level TASKS.json. Continue Stage 3 with task seeding or named adapters before Stage 11 MCP so MCP stays a thin interface over mature Python core behavior.
